term: so tāsaṁ vedanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti
preferred_translation: one does not discern those feelings' arising and vanishing, gratification, danger, and escape as they have come to be
definition: SN 36.6's untrained plural feeling-discernment line that leaves the ignorance tendency in place.
notes: Use this record for the untrained SN 36.6 plural-feelings line. Keep the five-part discernment structure aligned with MN 148 while preserving the plural reference to the three feeling registers.

example_phrases.pali: so tāsaṁ vedanānaṁ samudayañca atthaṅgamañca assādañca ādīnavañca nissaraṇañca yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti
example_phrases.translation: one does not discern those feelings' arising and vanishing, gratification, danger, and escape as they have come to be
example_phrases.notes: Untrained plural feeling-discernment line in SN 36.6.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the plural feeling-discernment line that leaves the ignorance tendency in place.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English control line for the untrained plural feeling-discernment formula in SN 36.6.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not flatten the line into generic understanding language or lose the five-part discernment structure.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the SN 36.6 plural line from drifting away from the governed gratification-danger-escape pattern.
